title: NS Micro Violin Tuner
comment:
This tuner is the fastest clip-on tuner on the market, period. It's certainly fast enough for playing scales at 140 bpm. There's no other clip-on, whether Hola, Korg, Snark, etc., that can do that.

The D'Addario literally blows away pedal tuners like Snark and Korg, which seem paralyzed compared to it.

Most importantly, it tunes accurately. I keep one parked on both of my violins. ??

title: A tech product ?? that works... out of the box!
comment:
[[VIDEOID:c9e3babe7624b0bdb09a2b49b68aa1dd]] What a refreshing experience. No configurations, no strange blinking pattern recognition challenge, no what the heck doesn't this thing work frustrations

How many videos have I taken where the subject can barely be heard or the wind noise overwhelms everything to make the video so awful it reeks of armature incompetence? Too many! The promise of these remote mics was so appealing, but would they actually work? How many obtuse Android parameters would I have to find to enable it? Would my phone and/or my camera support it? Probably not, but the allure was overwhelming so I anxiously awaited for my order to arrive.

I tore into the Amazon box with the enthusiasm of a kid at Christmas while fearing the disappointment sure to hit about the time I lay eyes on the user guide. Nothing much in the guide, so I plugged the receiver into the Type-C port on my phone and was immediately greeted with a message about phone switching to remote microphone. No way! It was within the hour that I had my wife out in the field testing this device out and pushing the limits to beyond any reasonable use case for remote audio. The video review tells all you need to know about sound quality, range, and wind resistance (it was almost a blizzard out there). The pictures on the listing make the mic look pretty big. In reality, they don't stand out any more than the evening news anchors do.

I gave this product 5 stars for living up to its claims. Herein, I would add another star for the very reasonable price, another star for actually exceeding expectations, and another for supporting Type-C directly with an adaptor for the proprietary Apple interface. That's what, 8 stars? Crazy, I know??

title: Order with Care - Bent """"T"""" bar"""
comment:
Pros: Decent for the money. Somewhat sturdy for holding laptops (not much heavier). Easy to assemble. Arrived fairly quickly.
Cons: Severely awful smell from packaging. Like others (oddly enough a guy with the same first name and last initial as me) a bent "T" bar. Flat top was textured but still slick. Questionable gluing or attachment of the angled end at bottom of flat top. I elected not to send the bent "t" bar unit back as the effort to re-pack and pay to ship back wouldn't be worth it. Overall if I had to buy it again I would not.
